data.title: A whale has deposited 58.6 million ASTER tokens into Binance, resulting in a total loss of over $23 million.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, a whale has been continuously depositing ASTER tokens into the Binance wallet of Daniel Larimer (@bytemaster7). To date, he has deposited 58.6 million ASTER tokens (worth approximately $92.3 million) into Binance at an average price of $1.57. He currently holds 14.1 million ASTER tokens (worth approximately $18 million), resulting in a total loss of over $23 million.

data.title: Whale Adjusts Ethereum Buy Order Amid Price Drop
data.description: According to BlockBeats, on-chain analyst Ai Yi (@ai_9684xtpa) has observed a significant adjustment by a whale in their Ethereum buy order. During the early hours, as Ethereum's price declined, the whale modified the price range for their limit buy order. The new target for purchasing Ethereum is set between $3,660 and $3,710, with a planned investment of $11 million. This adjustment reflects a $100 decrease from the previous order price.

data.title: Placeholder Partner: It is difficult to quickly form sustained buying after this round of crypto market plunge. We will consider buying again if BTC falls to $75,000 or lower
data.description: Chris Burniske, former head of crypto at Ark Invest and current partner at Placeholder VC, wrote on the X platform, "It's increasingly clear that last Friday's plunge has put the crypto market on hold in the short term. It's difficult to quickly generate sustained buying after such a crash. This cycle has been disappointing for most, which may be limiting action as everyone hopes for a 'market recovery' or previous all-time highs. It's easy to get caught up in the minutiae of charts, but looking at the monthly charts for BTC and ETH, it shows we're still in high range (albeit with cracks), which is ideal if you're considering taking profits. The MSTR is falling, gold is warning, as are credit markets, and stocks will be the last to react. We can always have a weak rebound, but I've already taken action (remember, cashing out is never all or nothing). I'll be watching BTC's reaction to $100,000, but I'd probably become interested again if it reaches $75,000 or lower. This bull market is different from the past, and the next bear market will be different too."

data.title: Caixin: Hong Kong crypto asset management company QMMM's stock has been suspended and its office is now empty.
data.description: According to Caixin, Nasdaq-listed QMMM Holdings (NASDAQ:QMMM) announced on September 9, 2025, plans to invest $100 million in a cryptocurrency reserve, sending its stock price soaring 9.6-fold within three weeks. At the end of September, the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) suspended trading of the company, effective September 29, alleging the company was suspected of manipulating its stock price through social media platforms. The suspension remains in effect. Caixin visited its Hong Kong headquarters on October 16th and found the office deserted. Upon contacting staff at another nearby company, they stated that the company had moved out in September and were unaware of the relocation. QMMM reportedly listed on Nasdaq in July 2024 and is registered in the Cayman Islands as a holding company. QMMM operates two subsidiaries: Manymany Creations and Quantum Matrix, both primarily engaged in media and animation production. Both are registered in Seaview Building near Tin Hau MTR Station in Hong Kong, and their registered business types are "advertising and market research."

data.title: Viewpoint: The main reasons for the US stock market correction are regional bank credit problems and the US government shutdown.
data.description: Crypto influencer @qinbafrank posted on the X platform that the recent US stock market correction is primarily due to two factors: 1. Credit problems at regional small and medium-sized banks. Zions Bancorp recorded a $50 million impairment loss on two commercial loans to its subsidiaries, while Western Alliance Bancorp sued Cantor Group V for loan fraud. Cantor Group V is a subsidiary of Cantor Fitzgerald, which helps manage Tether's US Treasury reserves. 2. The US government shutdown has led to liquidity constraints. Excessive bond issuance has depleted overnight reverse repo quotas, while the government shutdown has prevented the Treasury General Account (TGA) from disbursing funds, leading to a continued decline in commercial bank reserves and exacerbating market liquidity constraints. The analysis suggests that the market's future trajectory depends on when the shutdown ends.

data.title: Bitcoin Price Movements Could Trigger Significant Liquidations
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, data from Coinglass indicates that if Bitcoin's price falls below $107,000, the cumulative liquidation intensity of long positions on major centralized exchanges (CEX) will reach $729 million. Conversely, if Bitcoin surpasses $110,000, the cumulative liquidation intensity of short positions on these exchanges will amount to $850 million.

BlockBeats notes that the liquidation chart does not display the exact number of contracts pending liquidation or their precise value. Instead, the chart's bars represent the relative importance of each liquidation cluster compared to nearby clusters, indicating intensity.

Thus, the chart illustrates the potential impact on the market when the asset price reaches certain levels. A higher 'liquidation bar' suggests a more intense reaction due to liquidity waves when the price hits that point.

data.title: RWA project Elevexai deployed to MANTRA Chain
data.description: MANTRA announced on the X platform that its Real Estate Investment Exchange (RWA) project, Elevexai, was officially deployed to the MANTRA Chain earlier this week. Elevexai is a joint RWAccelerator (April 2025 batch) incubation project launched by MANTRA and Google Cloud. The project focuses on tokenizing real estate assets, with its core product being the Real Estate Investment Exchange (REIX), which provides tokenized trading services for real estate projects and indices.
